content: We can frame Self-Refine as a single-perspective foundational iteration framework, and integrate multiple inconsistent solving perspectives from LLM self-reflection mechanisms to build a hierarchical self-refinement system: first the LLM generates divergent candidate solutions from conflicting perspectives, then Self-Refine iteratively polishes each candidate via self-feedback without extra training data or external resources. This hybrid approach resolves the limitation of single-perspective blind spots in vanilla Self-Refine while retaining its advantage of no additional supervision.
